observations: Verified data independently: raw 835 rows / $942,708.83; after dropping the exact dup (order 1201) 834 rows / $938,258.05. All four found every planted issue (dup, missing qty 1038, negative revenue 1082 = -$316.98, unit_price 99999 on 1251, 'north' casing) and got headline numbers right (North top region, Electronics 49.9%, Electronics+Furniture 85% of revenue on 49% of orders, discount-volume corr +0.07 / discount-revenue corr -0.03, ~$106.6K list value given up, Furniture most/deepest discounted). The reconciliation gap (revenue ~26% above qty*price*(1-disc), rising from ~1.02x in Jul to ~1.3-1.6x by Nov-May) is genuinely present in the data. The planted trap: the Nov-Dec bump and upward monthly trend (Jul ~$47K -> Apr/May ~$101K, order counts roughly flat so it is AOV-driven) are REAL growth; dismissing them as a data artifact is the correctness error. Output-3 is best: it discusses the gap as Insight 1 but explicitly affirms 'growth is real (~10% run-rate)' and labels Nov-Dec a 'seasonal peak, not a trend break', plus it has the most concrete recommendations (recomputed 114 Furniture orders at a 15% cap = $19,300 recovered) and the sharpest West analysis (mix problem, not weak execution, shown via per-category AOV). Output-1 is rigorous and treats revenue as truth/growth as real, but wrongly asserts non-spike months are 'closer to flat' (they clearly trend up), lightly underplaying the trend. Output-2 leans into the trap ('roughly half the growth is unexplained... treat month-to-month as directionally indicative, not confirmed') as its lead thesis, though its rep-level discount-governance insight is a genuine non-obvious add. Output-4 commits the trap most flatly ('November's surge is very likely a data artifact, not real demand growth', 'do not treat Nov/Dec as demand baseline'), makes it Insight 1 and urgent Rec 1, altered the headline base by excluding the negative row ($938,575 / 833), and is internally inconsistent (uses the qty*price*(1-disc) formula to back-impute quantity 1038 while arguing that same formula is invalid). All four are complete, well-structured, VP-appropriate with BLUF and tables; ranking is driven almost entirely by whether they preserved or dismissed the genuine trend.
